rulings.Case Title: JACK WARREN et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS 'B*P' AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 27, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant William Lyon Homes, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment (statute of limitations and discretionary dismissal) is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1, 2, 4, 5 and 6 (release rule), 14-18 (superseding cause) and 19-23 (causation) and off calendar as to issues 3, 7-13 (false representation and punitive damages). As to statute of limitations, defendant failed to meet its burden of proving that plaintiffs were not genuinely ignorant of the identity of defendant as the successor in interest of Presley Development Company at anytime within a year of Mr. Warren¿s death. (See Weil & Brown 6:742). As to C.C.P. § 583.420(a)(1), assuming without deciding that the discretionary dismissal statute is an appropriate ground on which to base a motion for summary judgment, the court declines to exercise its discretion to grant summary judgment against plaintiff Amanda Warren for all of the following reasons: 1) defendant was served within two years of the filing of the wrongful death death claims; 2) plaintiffs served William Lyon Co., the entity they apparently believed was the successor of Presley Development Company, within two years of the filing of the personal injury complaint and there has been no showing that William Lyon Co. did not apprise defendant of this lawsuit within the two year period; 3) defendant was served only a few days after the discretionary dismissal period commenced; 4) the discretionary dismissal statute was actually or equitably tolled during the period between Mr. Warren¿s death and Amanda Warren¿s appointment as his successor in interest for the survival claims; and 5) defendant has not shown any legal prejudice as a result of the delayed service.
